GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Bulgari's latest jewellery collection is an homage to rock and rebellion", "And we're all for staging fine fashion mutiny.", "If there was ever a time to celebrate power and resilience, it is now. Fitting then, that for its newest line of fine jewels, B.zero1 Rock, Bulgari is celebrating the Italian house's bold Roman heritage. A city that boasts 2,700 years of art and architecture imbues Bulgari's iconic shapes with a fiercely modern edge. The unisex jewellery collection combines yellow gold with diamonds, black ceramic and studded finishes, and all of it pairs as perfectly with a silk dress as it does a leather jacket. Bulgari has leaned into creativity for this decidedly contemporary take on fine jewels. With a defiant fist in the air, the brand took inspiration from its heritage – the Bulgari Tubogas Chokers much beloved in the eighties, to inform strong jewels with a tough presence. Dainty diamond trim and studded centres turn the brand's celebrated classic ring shape into a full-scale statement piece sure to become a collector's piece. Pendants with geometric chains, bracelets and earrings all channel the same progressive aesthetic. The B.zero1 Rock collection was feted with a star-studded soiree earlier this year in New York City. Guests including Carolyn Murphy, Daphne Groeneveld, Lottie Moss, Jacob Elordi and David Gandy outfitted to the "More is more" dress code. Also in attendance was Vogue Australia's virtual style editor Gray Gannon, on her first assignment for the title. The glamorous crowd was entertained by high-energy sets from Ciara and Diplo. Befitting of the rebellious nature of the collection, the event also featured dynamic lighting effects, pyrotechnics and immersive experiential rooms. It was an evening which afforded Bulgari an opportunity to celebrate and unveil its latest muses. American actress and singer Zendaya was joined by model Lily Aldridge and British actress and singer Naomi Scott. They're the new faces of Bulgari's 2020 campaign titled Mai Troppo, shot in Bulgari's home city of Rome. B.zero1 Rock is heralding in a new era of precious meets powerful. What a revolution."
